def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    window = params['window']
    atr_multiplier = params['atr_multiplier']
    rsi_window = params['rsi_window']
    rsi_threshold = params['rsi_threshold']
    
    # Calculate support/resistance levels
    df['support'] = df['low'].rolling(window).min()
    df['resistance'] = df['high'].rolling(window).max()
    
    # Calculate ATR for volatility adjustment
    df['atr'] = ta.volatility.average_true_range(df['high'], df['low'], df['close'], window=window)
    
    # Calculate RSI for overbought/oversold conditions
    df['rsi'] = ta.momentum.rsi(df['close'], window=rsi_window)
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    
    # Long when price approaches support with low RSI
    long_condition = (df['close'] <= df['support'] + atr_multiplier * df['atr']) & (df['rsi'] < rsi_threshold)
    signals[long_condition] = 1
    
    # Short when price approaches resistance with high RSI
    short_condition = (df['close'] >= df['resistance'] - atr_multiplier * df['atr']) & (df['rsi'] > 100 - rsi_threshold)
    signals[short_condition] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: GBP/USD exhibits strong support/resistance levels where price reactions near these zones combined with RSI extremes provide high-probability reversal signals.
